prose.heading: Multi-site IPP cleaning, why one partner beats state-wise crews
prose.p1: Portfolio asset managers face inconsistent cleaning quality when every state uses a different labour contractor. Enterprise partnership standardises robots, SOPs, NECTYR reporting, and SLA response across plants, one governance model for Rajasthan dust and Karnataka humidity alike.
prose.p2: Taypro deploys on SECI, DVC, state discom, and IPP references from 50 MW to 360 MW single sites. Bachau DVC 300 MW (172 GLYDE), Neneva 250 MW trackers, and Akhadana 360 MW demonstrate robots-per-MW planning at institutional scale.
prose.p3: Commercial models flex per asset: OPEX on balance-sheet-sensitive holdings, CAPEX on core 20-year assets, hybrid transitions after proven PR recovery. Finance committees model both in the ROI calculator before portfolio master agreements.
prose.p4: Scope boundary remains clear: Taypro owns cleaning execution and fleet data. HV switching, inverter maintenance, and tracker mechanical work stay with your EPC or electrical O&M partner, we integrate via schedule coordination, not scope creep.

steps.heading: Enterprise partnership mobilisation, five phases
steps.subheading: From portfolio discovery to governed operations.
steps.step0Body: MW per site, states, installation mix, current cleaning spend, SECI/IPP context, and target PR assumptions.
steps.step1Body: CAPEX/OPEX per plant or blended enterprise rates, aligned to NECTYR measurement and SLA tiers.
steps.step2Body: Prioritise highest-soiling assets first; parallel mobilisation where service hubs exist.
steps.step3Body: Centralised scheduling, coverage audits, and monthly settlement across sites.
steps.step4Body: PR/soiling review, cycle adjustments, and ORION intelligence upgrade discussions at renewal.

eligibility.title: Who enterprise partnership is built for
eligibility.for0: IPP or CPSU portfolios with multiple utility sites 50 MW+
eligibility.for1: Asset managers needing one SLA and NECTYR audit trail across states
eligibility.for2: SECI or state tender programmes requiring documented cleaning coverage
eligibility.for3: Teams replacing fragmented state-wise manual contracts
eligibility.not0: Single small C&I rooftop without utility-scale sister plants
eligibility.not1: One-off annual wash without recurring robotic programme
eligibility.not2: Buyers seeking full-plant EPC O&M including HV and inverters

pillar0.title: Scale evidence
pillar0.body: Named 250–360 MW references, not pilot-scale marketing.

pillar1.title: Operational SLAs
pillar1.body: Breakdown response, spare parts, and remote diagnostics at portfolio level.

pillar2.title: Data governance
pillar2.body: NECTYR exports for lenders, internal audit, and SECI technical reviewers.

highlight.heading: The portfolio question
highlight.lead: Can one vendor govern cleaning across every state you operate in?
highlight.body: Enterprise partnership replaces fragmented crew contracts with one robotic fleet standard, one software layer, and one commercial counterparty.

projects.heading: Enterprise cleaning case studies by plant size
projects.subheading: 250–360 MW automatic robotic cleaning on IPP and CPSU plants in Gujarat and Rajasthan.

proseSecondary.heading: IPPs, CPSUs, and SECI programmes
proseSecondary.p1: Independent power producers optimise portfolio TCO and gearing, often mixing OPEX on new acquisitions and CAPEX on long-hold assets. CPSUs and SECI-tendered plants emphasise compliance evidence and pan-India vendor stability.
proseSecondary.p2: DVC and state discom references in Gujarat and Rajasthan demonstrate institutional procurement at 250–300 MW per site. Ask for robots-per-MW models and commissioning timelines in your enterprise RFQ.
proseSecondary.p3: Partnership discussions should include ORION roadmap for predictive O&M at AMC renewal, optional upgrade on Taypro-operated fleets.

faq.heading: Enterprise solar cleaning partnership, FAQ
faq.q0: What is an enterprise cleaning partnership?
faq.a0: A master agreement covering robots, operators, spares, and NECTYR across multiple utility sites, one SLA and reporting standard.
faq.a1: Typically multiple sites or single sites 50 MW+. Smaller one-off sites use standard service RFQ.
faq.q2: Can we mix CAPEX and OPEX across plants?
faq.a2: Yes. Enterprise frameworks often assign OPEX to new assets and CAPEX to long-hold core plants.
faq.q3: How does reporting work for lenders?
faq.a3: NECTYR block-level logs, coverage maps, and monthly panel-volume reconciliation per site and portfolio roll-up.
faq.a4: Breakdown response targets under 24 hours on enterprise fleets with regional spares, detailed in commercial proposal.
faq.a5: No. Cleaning and fleet data only. Partner with your EPC for HV, inverters, and trackers mechanical.
faq.a6: Cleaning SOPs and audit exports structured for technical due diligence, align to your tender checklist.
faq.a7: Akhadana 360 MW, Bachau DVC 300 MW, Neneva 250 MW: featured on this page and projects gallery.
faq.a8: Weeks per site depending on row count, phased on 200 MW+ plants. Proposed in enterprise proposal.
faq.a9: ORION early access discussed at AMC renewal for qualifying Taypro fleet customers.
faq.a10: Submit portfolio MW, states, and commercial model preference via contact form.
faq.a11: Service India is national overview. This page is multi-site enterprise contracting and governance.
